channel.title: MIT DCI – Curated Crypto News
channel.description: Weekly curated roundup of cryptocurrency and digital currency news from the MIT Media Lab Digital Currency Initiative.

item.title: SEC - SEC Staff Statement Regarding Broker-Dealer Registration of Certain User Interfaces Utilized to Prepare Transactions in Crypto Asset Securities
item.description: No-action framework for providers of certain crypto asset user interfaces.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • No-action framework for providers of certain crypto asset user interfaces.
  • Outlines conditions for operating without registering as broker-dealers under Section 15(a).
  • Applies to software (websites, apps, browser extensions) that helps users interact with blockchain protocols using self-custodial wallets.

See Also:

  • CoinDesk - U.S. SEC says software allowing crypto wallet transactions not considered broker

item.title: CryptoNews - U.S. CLARITY Act stablecoin bill faces May delay amid bank pushback
item.description: CLARITY Act's April committee review hangs in the balance as Senate Banking juggles Fed chair hearings and crypto legislation.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • CLARITY Act's April committee review hangs in the balance as Senate Banking juggles Fed chair hearings and crypto legislation.
  • Banking groups lobby hard against stablecoin yield, clashing with a White House report that pegs lending impact at just 0.02%.
  • White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt publicly calls banks "greedy or ignorant" as pressure mounts to stop stalling the bill.

See Also:

  • The Block - Clarity Act's stablecoin yield language pushed back; ban on idle balances still intact
  • CoinTelegraph - US senator urges delay of CLARITY Act Senate markup until May
  • The Block - List of 'unsolvable' issues have shrunk, top White House crypto advisory says as momentum builds to pass legislation

item.title: CoinTelegraph - SEC 'on the cusp' of onchain tokenized securities exemption: Atkins
item.description: SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the agency is nearing the release of an exemption for trading tokenized securities onchain.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the agency is nearing the release of an exemption for trading tokenized securities onchain.
  • Speaking at the Economic Club of Washington, described it as an exemption aimed at enabling limited activity in tokenized markets while longer-term rules develop.

item.title: CoinDesk - Western Union eyeing stablecoin launch to settle global transactions without SWIFT, CEO says
item.description: Western Union on track to launch USDPT stablecoin next month.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Western Union on track to launch USDPT stablecoin next month.
  • Will initially use USDPT behind the scenes as an alternative to SWIFT interbank network for real-time settlement.
  • Also developing a Digital Asset Network for crypto-to-fiat cash-outs and a Stable Card for holding stablecoins.

item.title: The Defiant - New York Attorney General Sues Coinbase, Gemini Over Unlicensed Prediction Markets
item.description: NY AG Letitia James sued Coinbase and Gemini on April 21, alleging both illegally operated unlicensed gambling operations through prediction markets.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • NY AG Letitia James sued Coinbase and Gemini on April 21, alleging both illegally operated unlicensed gambling operations through prediction markets.
  • Investigation found prediction markets allowing bets on sports, entertainment, and election outcomes violate NY state gambling laws.

item.title: The Block - Bipartisan PACE Act looks to open Fed payment rails to nonbanks, draws crypto support
item.description: Bill aims to make payments faster and cheaper.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Bill aims to make payments faster and cheaper.
  • Blockchain Association CEO Summer Mersinger called the bill an "important step forward."

item.title: BBC - Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
item.description: Justin Sun has accused World Liberty Financial of an "illegal scheme" to seize his WLFI tokens.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Justin Sun has accused World Liberty Financial of an "illegal scheme" to seize his WLFI tokens.
  • Sun alleges the firm has "frozen" all of his tokens and stripped him of his right to vote on governance issues.

item.title: PYMNTS - BIS Report Warns Banking Services From Crypto Firms Carry Risk
item.description: Global banking regulator warns of risks from crypto exchanges offering banking services.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Global banking regulator warns of risks from crypto exchanges offering banking services.
  • Services from "multifunction cryptoasset intermediaries" (MCIs) lack protections of traditional financial institutions.

item.title: FinTech News SG - BIS Official Says Stablecoins Still Fall Short as Widely Used Payment Instrument
item.description: Pablo Hernandez de Cos warned wider use of stablecoins could create policy challenges from credit provision to monetary policy.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Pablo Hernandez de Cos warned wider use of stablecoins could create policy challenges from credit provision to monetary policy.
  • Stablecoins offer features like programmability and atomic settlement but remain limited in their broader financial role.

item.title: The Block - Circle hit with class action lawsuit over alleged inaction in $280 million Drift exploit
item.description: Circle facing lawsuit from Drift Protocol investors who lost money in the $280M exploit on April 1.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Circle facing lawsuit from Drift Protocol investors who lost money in the $280M exploit on April 1.
  • Lawsuit alleges Circle failed to act swiftly to freeze stolen USDC.

item.title: Reuters - Circle CEO sees 'tremendous opportunity' for yuan-backed stablecoin
item.description: Jeremy Allaire says stablecoins are a good way to "export" a currency.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Jeremy Allaire says stablecoins are a good way to "export" a currency.
  • Thinks China could launch a yuan-backed stablecoin within three to five years.

item.title: CoinDesk - Circle CEO says he won't freeze USDC without a court order even as hackers walk away with millions
item.description: Jeremy Allaire said Circle freezes USDC wallets only when directed by law enforcement or courts, not in real time during hacks.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Jeremy Allaire said Circle freezes USDC wallets only when directed by law enforcement or courts, not in real time during hacks.
  • Critics argue claim delays have allowed over $420 million in illicit funds to escape since 2022.

item.title: Decrypt via Yahoo Finance - KelpDAO Bridge Hack Pinned on North Korea's Lazarus Group
item.description: $292M exploit drained from KelpDAO's cross-chain bridge, likely by North Korea's Lazarus Group (TraderTraitor subunit).
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • $292M exploit drained from KelpDAO's cross-chain bridge, likely by North Korea's Lazarus Group (TraderTraitor subunit).
  • Attackers drained 116,500 rsETH, setting off withdrawals across DeFi that pulled more than $10 billion out of Aave.

See Also:

  • CoinDesk - Crypto's massive exploit may force big banks to rethink their blockchain plans, Jefferies warns
  • CoinDesk - Aave raises nearly 80% of the $200 million it needs to cover bad debt left by Kelp DAO exploit
  • LayerZero - KelpDAO Incident Statement

item.title: The Block - Kevin Warsh discloses investments in a slew of crypto firms
item.description: Kevin Warsh disclosed holdings including investments in Polymarket and over 20 different crypto-linked entities.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Kevin Warsh disclosed holdings including investments in Polymarket and over 20 different crypto-linked entities.

item.title: The Block - Researcher breaks 15-bit elliptic curve key in 'largest quantum attack,' wins 1 bitcoin bounty from Project Eleven
item.description: Giancarlo Lelli derived a 15-bit elliptic curve key on a publicly accessible quantum computer.
item.content:encoded:
[Top News] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Giancarlo Lelli derived a 15-bit elliptic curve key on a publicly accessible quantum computer.
  • Largest quantum attack on elliptic curve cryptography to date, awarded 1 BTC bounty.
  • However, it remains far below real-world cryptographic standards.

item.title: FT - How worried should Wall St be about the stablecoin threat to deposits?
item.description: Upcoming Clarity Act seeks to resolve whether stablecoin issuers can offer interest-like rewards, impacting a market projected to grow from $300B to $3T by 2030.
item.content:encoded:
[Interviews / Podcasts / Top Thought Pieces] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Upcoming Clarity Act seeks to resolve whether stablecoin issuers can offer interest-like rewards, impacting a market projected to grow from $300B to $3T by 2030.
  • While bankers fear stablecoins will drain deposits, the threat is currently limited because stablecoins lack transactional convenience.

item.title: CoinTelegraph - Stablecoin transfer volume drops 19% even as supply keeps rising
item.description: Monthly transfer volume fell nearly 20% over the past 30 days, even as total supply and holder count continued to rise.
item.content:encoded:
[Interviews / Podcasts / Top Thought Pieces] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Monthly transfer volume fell nearly 20% over the past 30 days, even as total supply and holder count continued to rise.
  • 30-day transfer volume dropped 19.18% to $8.31 trillion; market cap rose 2.06% to $305.29 billion.

item.title: The ValueExchange with Digital Asset - Tokenised collateral goes global
item.description: Cross-border markets cause ~$6T in collateral to sit idle every night, resulting in $1.2B in lost interest earnings daily.
item.content:encoded:
[Interviews / Podcasts / Top Thought Pieces] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Cross-border markets cause ~$6T in collateral to sit idle every night, resulting in $1.2B in lost interest earnings daily.
  • Live repo trades expanded to include UK Gilts and European Government Bonds across ten time zones and three jurisdictions.

item.title: Forbes - 'Rails Have Shifted' - Stablecoins Topped ACH At $7.5 Trillion A Month
item.description: Monthly stablecoin settlement volume has surpassed ACH network for the first time, reaching $7.5 trillion.
item.content:encoded:
[Interviews / Podcasts / Top Thought Pieces] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Monthly stablecoin settlement volume has surpassed ACH network for the first time, reaching $7.5 trillion.
  • USDC now leads in adjusted transaction volume at ~70% of activity due to GENIUS Act compliance.
  • Onchain dollar settlement projections could reach $719 trillion by 2035.

item.title: FEDS Notes - Stablecoins in 2025: Developments and Financial Stability Implications
item.description: During 2025, stablecoins grew by about 50% in market cap, with transaction volume and DeFi use surging.
item.content:encoded:
[Interviews / Podcasts / Top Thought Pieces] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • During 2025, stablecoins grew by about 50% in market cap, with transaction volume and DeFi use surging.
  • Stablecoins with safer and more liquid reserve composition have exhibited relatively stronger adoption.

item.title: ECB - Tokenised bonds: assessing efficiency and liquidity in a nascent market
item.description: Investigates empirically whether tokenisation of bonds improves bond issuance efficiency and market liquidity.
item.content:encoded:
[Interviews / Podcasts / Top Thought Pieces] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Investigates empirically whether tokenisation of bonds improves bond issuance efficiency and market liquidity.

item.title: The Block - Ondo seeks SEC clearance for tokenized equities model on Ethereum
item.description: The SEC is signaling openness to tokenization, encouraging firms to engage directly.
item.content:encoded:
[US] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • The SEC is signaling openness to tokenization, encouraging firms to engage directly.
  • Tokenized RWAs total about $23 billion today, projections see growth into the trillions.

item.title: PYMNTS - FASB Wants Companies to Reveal Stablecoin Holdings
item.description: FASB voted to propose that all companies each year reveal stablecoin holdings as part of cash equivalents disclosure.
item.content:encoded:
[US] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • FASB voted to propose that all companies each year reveal stablecoin holdings as part of cash equivalents disclosure.

item.title: Asia Asset Management - Hong Kong sets high bar for stablecoin issuance with first licences
item.description: HKMA awarded its first stablecoin issuer licences to HSBC and a Standard Chartered Bank-led consortium on April 10.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • HKMA awarded its first stablecoin issuer licences to HSBC and a Standard Chartered Bank-led consortium on April 10.
  • Decision concluded a long regulatory process following HKMA's first discussion paper in January 2022.

item.title: The Block - Hong Kong warns of fake tokens posing as HSBC's stablecoin
item.description: HKMA warned that tokens named "HKDAP" and "HSBC" are not associated with any licensed stablecoin issuers.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • HKMA warned that tokens named "HKDAP" and "HSBC" are not associated with any licensed stablecoin issuers.

item.title: FinTech News HK - SFC Opens Secondary Trading of Tokenised Products in Hong Kong Under New Framework
item.description: SFC launched a framework to pilot secondary trading of tokenised SFC-authorised investment products.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • SFC launched a framework to pilot secondary trading of tokenised SFC-authorised investment products.
  • Initial batch will focus on tokenised money market funds.

item.title: CoinTelegraph - EU adviser says 'MiCA 2' is likely as crypto market matures
item.description: MiCA review is part of an effort to ensure rules keep pace with a changing market structure.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • MiCA review is part of an effort to ensure rules keep pace with a changing market structure.

item.title: The Block - Russia passes crypto bill in first reading; permits use in foreign trade settlements
item.description: Russia's State Duma passed a crypto bill to establish a framework for crypto circulation.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Russia's State Duma passed a crypto bill to establish a framework for crypto circulation.
  • Prohibits domestic payments but allows use in foreign economic activities.

item.title: The Block - French minister urges banks to expand euro stablecoins, tokenized deposits
item.description: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure urged European banks to expand euro-pegged stablecoins and explore tokenized deposits.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • French Finance Minister Roland Lescure urged European banks to expand euro-pegged stablecoins and explore tokenized deposits.

item.title: CoinTelegraph - EU sanctions target Russian crypto exchanges, stablecoins and CBDC
item.description: European Commission announced a total sectorial ban on carrying out exchanges with any Russian crypto asset service provider.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • European Commission announced a total sectorial ban on carrying out exchanges with any Russian crypto asset service provider.

item.title: CoinTelegraph - Canada proposes crypto ATM ban over scams and money laundering
item.description: Canadian government proposed banning Bitcoin and other crypto ATMs, calling them a "primary method for scammers to defraud victims."
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Canadian government proposed banning Bitcoin and other crypto ATMs, calling them a "primary method for scammers to defraud victims."

item.title: CoinTelegraph - Canada advances bill to ban crypto political donations
item.description: Bill C-25, the Strong and Free Elections Act, passed a second reading in the House of Commons.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Bill C-25, the Strong and Free Elections Act, passed a second reading in the House of Commons.

item.title: The Block - UK sets out plan to integrate payments rules covering stablecoins and tokenized deposits
item.description: UK Treasury announced a regulatory package to consolidate stablecoins and tokenized deposits into a single framework alongside traditional payment services.
item.content:encoded:
[International] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • UK Treasury announced a regulatory package to consolidate stablecoins and tokenized deposits into a single framework alongside traditional payment services.

item.title: Decrypt - Circle 'Exploring' Arc Network Token Launch, Proof-of-Stake Shift
item.description: Circle is "exploring" a token for Arc Network, its stablecoin-focused layer-1 blockchain.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Circle is "exploring" a token for Arc Network, its stablecoin-focused layer-1 blockchain.
  • Mainnet beta launch earmarked for 2026.

item.title: Circle - Circle Skills Launch on GitHub
item.description: Instructional framework (Skills) to help AI agents understand best practices for building with USDC.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Instructional framework (Skills) to help AI agents understand best practices for building with USDC.
  • Covers decision frameworks (e.g. CCTP vs. Gateway), correct patterns (6-decimal precision), and error prevention.

item.title: Decrypt - Tether Asserts Stablecoin Dominance Over Circle's USDC Amid Major Crypto Hacks
item.description: USDT market cap growth has outpaced USDC since Drift Protocol was exploited for $285 million.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • USDT market cap growth has outpaced USDC since Drift Protocol was exploited for $285 million.
  • Nansen analyst posited that Tether's stablecoin likely offers superior liquidity during DeFi crises.

item.title: The Block - Tether backs Stablecoin Development Corporation's $134 million private placement used to acquire SKY tokens
item.description: Tether supported SDEV's $134M private placement in January. SDEV holds SKY governance tokens and uses Sky's USDS stablecoin.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Tether supported SDEV's $134M private placement in January. SDEV holds SKY governance tokens and uses Sky's USDS stablecoin.

item.title: CoinTelegraph - Tether launches self-custodial wallet with cloud backup option
item.description: Tether launched tether.wallet supporting USDT, XAUt (XAUT), US-focused USAT, and Bitcoin.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Tether launched tether.wallet supporting USDT, XAUt (XAUT), US-focused USAT, and Bitcoin.

item.title: PYMNTS - Tether Freezes $334 Million in Stablecoins Linked to Illegal Activity
item.description: Tether froze $344 million in stablecoins on April 23, following information from U.S. authorities.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Tether froze $344 million in stablecoins on April 23, following information from U.S. authorities.

item.title: The Block - Tempo unveils pragmatic privacy solution called Zones
item.description: Each Zone is a private execution environment managed by a trusted entity like a financial institution.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Each Zone is a private execution environment managed by a trusted entity like a financial institution.
  • Designed for institutional use cases like payroll, treasury, and settlements.

item.title: The Block - Visa, Stripe and Zodia Custody by Standard Chartered become validators on Tempo's payments blockchain
item.description: Notable early additions for Tempo as it builds out its validator set alongside an agentic payments protocol.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Notable early additions for Tempo as it builds out its validator set alongside an agentic payments protocol.

item.title: Decrypt via Yahoo Finance - DoorDash to Pay Delivery Workers in Stablecoins via Stripe's Tempo Blockchain
item.description: DoorDash will use Tempo to pay delivery workers with stablecoins in more than 40 countries.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • DoorDash will use Tempo to pay delivery workers with stablecoins in more than 40 countries.

item.title: CoinDesk - Tech giant Meta starts paying some creators in stablecoin with Stripe's support
item.description: Meta began rolling out stablecoin payouts to select creators in USDC on Solana or Polygon.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Meta began rolling out stablecoin payouts to select creators in USDC on Solana or Polygon.
  • Marks Meta's renewed push into stablecoin payments after Libra/Diem was shut down.

item.title: PYMNTS - Fireblocks Intros Stablecoin Lending for Institutional Clients
item.description: Fireblocks debuted Earn tool letting institutional customers lend stablecoins to Morpho and Aave protocols.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Fireblocks debuted Earn tool letting institutional customers lend stablecoins to Morpho and Aave protocols.

item.title: Fireblocks - Major European Bank Consortium Qivalis Plans to Leverage Fireblocks to Power MiCAR-compliant Euro-denominated Stablecoin
item.description: Twelve major European banks creating a MiCAR-compliant euro-backed stablecoin, launching second half of 2026.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Twelve major European banks creating a MiCAR-compliant euro-backed stablecoin, launching second half of 2026.
  • Banks include Banca Sella, BBVA, BNP Paribas, CaixaBank, Danske Bank, DekaBank, DZ BANK, ING, KBC, Raiffeisen, SEB, and UniCredit.

item.title: PYMNTS - Visa Adds 5 Blockchains as Stablecoin Settlement Volume Surges
item.description: Visa adding Arc, Base, Canton, Polygon and Tempo, bringing total to nine supported blockchains.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Visa adding Arc, Base, Canton, Polygon and Tempo, bringing total to nine supported blockchains.

item.title: The Block - Morgan Stanley launches stablecoin reserves fund aligned with GENIUS Act requirements
item.description: Government money market fund maintaining stable $1 NAV, investing in cash and U.S. Treasury instruments with maturities of 93 days or less.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Government money market fund maintaining stable $1 NAV, investing in cash and U.S. Treasury instruments with maturities of 93 days or less.

item.title: FinExtra - SocGen brings stablecoin to MetaMask wallet users
item.description: Societe Generale's SG-Forge joining forces with Consensys to expand access to its USD CoinVertible stablecoin via MetaMask.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Societe Generale's SG-Forge joining forces with Consensys to expand access to its USD CoinVertible stablecoin via MetaMask.
  • First regulated stablecoin issued by a major European bank available in MetaMask.

item.title: The Block - Western Union to launch stablecoin next month, with 'Stable Card' planned for global consumers
item.description: USDPT launch confirmed for next month plus a Digital Asset Network and stablecoin payment card.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • USDPT launch confirmed for next month plus a Digital Asset Network and stablecoin payment card.

item.title: Nala - MoneyGram and NALA Partner for Stablecoin Settlement and Modernized Payouts Across Emerging Markets
item.description: Strategic partnership to power cross-border payouts into emerging markets using stablecoin-based settlement infrastructure.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • Strategic partnership to power cross-border payouts into emerging markets using stablecoin-based settlement infrastructure.

item.title: CoinDesk - eToro to acquire crypto wallet Zengo in $70 million deal
item.description: eToro moving into self-custody with Zengo acquisition, targeting tokenized assets and decentralized trading models.
item.content:encoded:
[Company Updates] — Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026

  • eToro moving into self-custody with Zengo acquisition, targeting tokenized assets and decentralized trading models.
